props.message:
BURNT HOLLOW.

The wood that grew here was old when the Source first sang. Khorgaul the Hoarder burned it to its roots and locked the ash in glass. Walk softly. The ground remembers.
Where the ridge narrows: keep your blade drawn. The bone-walkers know every crack of this stone.

A Source-shrine, half-melted into glass slag.

Here Lirien wept, and her tears boiled. The hoarder bled the Source dry into this hollow. Embers still bank in the cracks.
Beyond: the dead-end shelf. The Hoarder's reliquary lies on the far stone — but the path narrows to a bone's-width.
Knights of bone do not forget where their hoard lies. Take only what you can carry. Leave the rest to ash.

props.open_message: A small iron key, scorched but whole. Khorgaul hoarded a thousand such keys; this one knows a lock somewhere far from here.
A bundle of black-powder bombs. Press F to throw one. Cracked stone fears the spark.
A piece of the Source — a heart-ember, banked in cracked glass. It warms you back to life.
A scatter of pebbles — fifteen good throwing-stones. The bone-knights notice.

root.speaker: Soot Helka
root.text:
*coughs ash*
You — you came in. Most don't, anymore. I can't go back. The fire ate the ridge behind me. Look — look how the Hollow weeps ash.

what.speaker: Soot Helka
what.text: The wood was old when the Source was young. Then Khorgaul gathered the green out of it — not at once, no, that would be merciful — gathered it slow, gathered it patient, gathered it gone. The Source bleeds. You can taste it on the air.

khor.speaker: Soot Helka
khor.text: He does not show himself. He sends bone-things and ash-things and the cold beneath. But this — *gestures at the burnt land* — this is his hand. As clear as a print in clay.

deep.speaker: Soot Helka
deep.text: His reliquary. Where he hoards what he has gathered. The bone-knights guard it; the bats keep its sky. Go in only if you can come out. Few can. None alone, I think.

bye.speaker: Soot Helka
bye.text: Go bright. Go careful. Go and come back.
